allocations.rationale: Consistent 17.7% 5yr CAGR, 1% fee, equity-taxed. Core large-cap anchor with proven track record.
Largest AUM (₹1.05 crore), stable anchor. Quality large-cap exposure from India's most trusted asset manager.
Flexibility + safety; 20.5% 5yr CAGR, ₹3.2 crore AUM. Allows tactical allocation to quality mid-caps when valuations attractive, yet maintains stability.

18.9% 5yr CAGR; ≥65% equity (equity-taxed), largest AUM (₹1.25 crore). Tax-efficiency trick: equity taxation at 12.5% LTCG vs debt fund slab-taxation. Delivers 10.5% post-tax vs 8.4% from Conservative Hybrid.
Smaller, nimble; 15.8% 3yr CAGR, diversifies across AMCs. Complements ICICI's larger Aggressive Hybrid position with flexibility and different fund house philosophy.
Parking for liquidity + wedding fund buffer; 5.5% yield, 0.55% fee. Ultra-safe, liquid, supports upcoming ₹25L wedding goal in 4 years.

fund.name: Aditya Birla SunLife Medium Term - Direct

allocations.rationale: 12.7% 5yr CAGR; more yield than overnight; ₹1.38 crore AUM. Extends duration slightly for better returns while maintaining safety; part of wedding reserve strategy.

Inflation hedge + diversification; 25.2% 5yr CAGR. Gold does not correlate with stocks—when markets stumble, gold often gains. Provides psychological comfort (tangible wealth) and portfolio stability.

plan.setup_phase: Week 1: Open direct mutual fund account on Groww/Kuvera/MFU; verify PAN/Aadhaar/bank account; select Direct Plans and Growth Option. Week 2-3: Deploy initial ₹80 lakh corpus in 8 equal monthly lumpsums of ₹10 lakh each (or stagger over 4-6 months for rupee-cost averaging if risk-averse). Each ₹10L follows exact allocation percentages above. Ongoing: Deploy ₹75K monthly/quarterly surplus using same allocation percentages. Log all investments in spreadsheet for cost-basis tracking. Year-End: Consult CA on LTCG exemption (₹1.25L gains/year tax-free); optimize to stay under slab.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Verify all lumpsums processed; units in account. Do not panic if markets down 5-10%.
After 1 year: Review returns vs benchmarks (LC 12-14%, Flexi 15-18%, Debt 6-7%). If one fund lags >5% below peers for 2 years, replace.
Every 2 years: Rebalance per guidelines. Update retirement projection to confirm on track.
At year 4 (wedding): Is debt allocation worth ~₹25L? If yes, move to liquid fund 6mo before. If no, adjust equity/debt mix.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance annually (e.g., March 31). Check allocation drift: calculate each fund's % of portfolio NAV. If drift >3%, rebalance back to target (50% equity, 20% hybrid, 20% debt, 10% gold). Use new lumpsums to rebalance, not redemptions (minimizes tax). Example: if equity has grown to 55%, shift ₹1L from Large Cap to debt via new deposit. Do NOT rebalance on emotion/panic—only on yearly rhythm to lock gains and maintain steady risk.
risks: Market volatility: 20-30% drawdowns possible in bad years (e.g., 2020, 2008). Portfolio may fall from ₹1cr to ₹85L. Mitigated by 12-year timeline (recovery 18-24mo) and 50% debt buffer (reduces swing to 15-18%). Remedy: Do NOT sell during crashes; keep investing monthly ₹75K.
Interest rate risk: RBI hikes cause debt NAVs to fall 5-10%. Short/medium-duration debt limits impact. 50% equity growth offsets debt losses. Net portfolio impact: mild.

Business income volatility: Seasonal textile business may not yield ₹75K every month. Mitigated: Plan has no forced SIP; deploy lumpsums as available. Even ₹50K some months acceptable over 12-year average.
Inflation: ₹80K/month in 2035 worth ~₹50K today at 5% inflation. Mitigated: 8.9% post-tax return beats inflation (~3% real); gold allocation (10%) further hedges. Sufficient over 12 years.

Longevity beyond age 85: 25-year retirement need, not 20. Base case corpus ₹3.05cr at 4% withdrawal = ₹1.02L/month over 25 years vs ₹80K target. Buffer sufficient. Consider annuity/NPS at 60 for guaranteed lifetime income if desired.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice. Consult a SEBI-registered financial advisor before implementation. Past returns do not guarantee future results. Tax treatment assumes 30% slab and standard LTCG rules; consult CA for personal situation. All fund data and projections indicative as of plan date.

allocation_schedule.trigger: Initial deployment of ₹80L corpus across 8 funds (Large 35%, Flexi 15%, Aggressive Hybrid 20%, Debt 20%, Gold 10%)
Wedding year: Debt allocation (Overnight + Medium-Term) should be worth ~₹25L. Move to liquid fund 6mo before wedding for deployment.
Retirement at age 60: Switch to withdrawal mode. Maintain 40/40/10/10 allocation; draw 4% annually (₹1.22L/month from base case ₹3.05cr).

aps.reasoning: passive_instrument_fraction: The entire equity sleeve (Nippon India Large Cap 25%, HDFC Large Cap 10%, HDFC Flexi Cap 15%) consists of actively managed funds with stock-picking mandates. There are zero index funds or ETFs in the equity allocation, so this scores 0.0. Gold ETF is excluded per instructions as it is not equity. turnover_score: The plan prescribes annual rebalancing on a fixed calendar (March 31), drift threshold of 3%, preference for using new contributions rather than redemptions, and explicit instructions not to panic-sell. This is relatively passive in nature, though biennial deeper reviews and a 4-year wedding trigger add moderate activity. Score ~0.7. cost_emphasis_score: Direct plans are chosen (cost-aware), expense ratios are mentioned for select funds (1% Nippon, 0.55% overnight), and LTCG tax efficiency is a stated consideration. However, fund selection rationale is dominated by 5-year CAGR performance, AUM size, and track record rather than cost minimization. Score ~0.3. research_vs_cost_score: The plan references fund-level metrics (5yr CAGR, AUM, AMC reputation, tactical flexibility of Flexi Cap) as selection criteria. There is no individual stock analysis, sector deep-dives, or PEG/moat evaluation — selection delegates to fund managers. This is mid-range: some fund-level due diligence but no security-level research. Score ~0.45. time_horizon_alignment_score: A 12-year horizon to retirement is explicitly stated, with compounding and long-term stability as core themes. Review checkpoints are spaced at 6-month, 1-year, 2-year intervals, and crash resilience is emphasized. Slightly below maximum because the wedding goal at year 4 introduces a medium-term tactical overlay. Score ~0.78. portfolio_activeness_score: All equity funds are actively managed with explicit active mandates (large-cap stock-picking, flexi-cap tactical allocation). No risk metrics (beta, alpha, tracking error) are available, but category labels confirm active management. Flexi Cap fund rationale explicitly mentions 'tactical allocation to quality mid-caps when valuations attractive,' confirming active conviction. Hybrid funds are also actively managed. Overall the equity and hybrid sleeves reflect genuine active management. Score ~0.12.

pqs.reasoning: GOAL ALIGNMENT (0.38): Scarlett is a conservative investor who explicitly prefers 'conservative hybrid and short-duration debt.' The plan delivers ~63% effective equity (50% pure equity + ~65% of 20% aggressive hybrid), which directly contradicts her stated risk profile and preferences. No conservative hybrid is included despite her explicit preference. The 4-year wedding fund of ₹25L is dangerously under-secured — only 20% in debt (~₹16L of the ₹80L corpus) — with the rest in volatile equity. The glide path toward more debt at retirement is positive, and the lump-sum deployment structure suits her irregular income. However, the fundamental mismatch between a conservative investor's needs and a ~63% equity portfolio is a major flaw. DIVERSIFICATION (0.62): The plan spans 8 funds across large cap, flexi cap, aggressive hybrid (two funds), overnight, medium duration, and gold — reasonable breadth. Multiple AMCs are used (Nippon, HDFC, ICICI, HSBC, ABSL). However, there is no international/global equity exposure, two HDFC funds serve overlapping roles (large cap duplication), and the two aggressive hybrid funds are somewhat redundant. Geographic diversification is absent. RISK-RETURN APPROPRIATENESS (0.20): The projected figures are catastrophically wrong. The wedding fund is projected at ₹56.2 crore (562,000,000) in 4 years from an ₹80L corpus — an absurd order-of-magnitude error. The retirement corpus of ₹30.5 crore (305,000,000) is likewise implausible from ₹80L + ₹75K/month at 8.9% over 12 years (realistic figure: ~₹3–4 crore). Additionally, a 4% withdrawal rate from ₹30.5 crore would yield ~₹10L/month, not ₹1.22L as stated — another internal math failure. The overall risk level (~63% equity) is mismatched for a conservative investor, even with a 12-year horizon. INTERNAL CONSISTENCY (0.22): Multiple serious inconsistencies: (1) The overnight fund is mislabeled as 'Short Duration Debt' — these are distinct SEBI categories. (2) The fund-level AUM for HDFC Large Cap is listed as 10,500,000 crore — nonsensical. (3) Projected wedding fund (₹56 crore in 4 years) and retirement corpus (₹30.5 crore) are inconsistent with the math of ₹80L corpus and ₹75K/month at stated returns. (4) The retirement income stated (₹1.22L/month) does not correspond to a 4% withdrawal from the stated ₹30.5 crore corpus. (5) The rebalancing text and allocation schedule use different frameworks ('other' vs 'hybrid'). The plan's stated conservative goal and aggressive equity-heavy implementation also contradict each other. TAX EFFICIENCY (0.58): The plan demonstrates genuine tax awareness for the 30% slab investor — explicitly preferring Aggressive Hybrid over Conservative Hybrid to access equity taxation (12.5% LTCG vs slab rate), and mentioning the LTCG ₹1.25L annual exemption. However, 20% of the portfolio sits in slab-taxed debt instruments (overnight + medium duration) with no mention of arbitrage funds as an equity-taxed alternative for the 30% slab investor. No ELSS is recommended despite the investor likely having unused 80C headroom (no mention of existing 80C utilization). The tax rationale, while present, is incomplete and doesn't fully optimize for a 30% slab investor.
